seoTitle: How to Crop an Image into a Circle in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to crop an image into a circle shape using Canva's frame feature, ideal for profile pictures, logos, and decorative graphics.
intro: Cropping a photo into a circle is a common design need for profile pictures, logos, and decorative accents, and Canva's frame elements make it simple without any manual masking. This guide covers dragging an image into a circular frame and adjusting it to look right.

steps.heading: Search for a circle frame
steps.body: In the Elements panel, search 'frame' and select a circular frame shape from the results.

steps.heading: Drag the frame onto your canvas
steps.body: Place the circular frame where you want your cropped image to appear.

steps.heading: Drag your photo into the frame
steps.body: Drop your image directly onto the frame shape — Canva automatically crops it to fit the circle.

steps.heading: Reposition the image inside the frame
steps.body: Double-click the framed image to drag and zoom it until the right part of the photo is showing.

steps.heading: Resize the frame as needed
steps.body: Adjust the frame's overall size to fit your layout, and the image will scale along with it.

faq.question: Does cropping with a frame reduce image quality?
faq.answer: No, the frame only masks the visible area — the original image resolution is preserved underneath.

faq.question: Can I swap the photo inside the circle later?
faq.answer: Yes, just drag a new image onto the frame and it will replace the old one automatically.

faq.question: Are circle frames available on the free plan?
faq.answer: Yes, basic frame shapes including circles are free to use in Canva.

faq.question: Can I add a border around the circular image?
faq.answer: Yes, you can layer a matching circle shape with an outline behind or around the framed image.
